src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/pa.general_idea: Replace Optional.isPresent() with Optional.isEmpty() for better readability and modern API usage.

src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/sp.general_idea: Replace the negated isPresent() check with the isEmpty() method introduced in Java 21 for Optional.
src/main/java/com/bernardomg/security/sp.why_exists: The method isEmpty() was added in Java 21; while isPresent() still exists, this change is part of a migration recipe to adopt the new API style, but strictly speaking, isPresent() is not removed in Java 21. However, the prompt implies this is a migration to J21. Let's re-read the rules. 'bucket=breaking ONLY if the migration won't compile or run on the target JDK without this change.' Since isPresent() is NOT removed in Java 21, this change is NOT required for compilation. It is a stylistic update/polishment to use the new API. Wait, looking at the recipe name 'UpgradeToJava21', often these recipes enforce new APIs. But is it breaking? No. isPresent() works in J21. So it is polishment.

src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.general_idea: Replace iterator().next() with getFirst() to use the new List API introduced in Java 21.
src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.why_exists: iterator().next() is valid in Java 17 but getFirst() is the preferred modern API in Java 21; however, since iterator().next() still compiles and runs on Java 21, this is a polishment change driven by the migration recipe to adopt new APIs.

src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.general_idea: Replace iterator().next() with getFirst() to use the new List API introduced in Java 21.
src/test/java/com/bernardomg/association.why_exists: The method getFirst() does not exist on List in Java 17, causing a compilation error.
